forecasts.question: Will AGI be developed before 1 January 2030?
forecasts.key_change: No significant change; consensus still sceptical of near-term AGI despite Musk claims. Shane Legg gives 50% by 2028; community predictions ~10% for 2026.

factors_up.factor: Accelerating capability gains across labs
Shane Legg gives 50% by 2028
Massive compute buildout continuing

factors_down.factor: No lab has publicly claimed AGI
Stanford experts predict no AGI in 2026
Scaling showing diminishing returns in some benchmarks

key_uncertainties: Whether current architectures can achieve AGI-level generalisation

update_triggers.up: Major lab announces system passing comprehensive AGI benchmarks
update_triggers.down: Prominent scaling plateaus or lab pivots

forecasts.question: Will economic consultancy lose ≥33% value/jobs by end 2030 due to AI?
forecasts.key_change: Slight uptick (+1pp): McKinsey planning 10% layoffs, industry-wide cuts accelerating. But 33% threshold remains very high for 4.5 years.
forecasts.base_rate: Historical industry disruptions rarely cause >33% decline in <5 years

factors_up.factor: McKinsey 10% headcount reduction
65% of enterprises say traditional consulting no longer provides enough value
AI enterprise revenue surging

factors_down.factor: Industry pivoting to AI implementation services
Execution-centric firms growing at 2x strategy firms
33% is an extremely high bar; current layoffs 5-10%

key_uncertainties: Speed of AI agent replacement of analyst-level work
Whether revenue shifts count as loss if firms pivot

update_triggers.up: Major firm reports >20% revenue decline attributed to AI
update_triggers.down: Consulting firms show revenue growth from AI services

forecasts.question: Will Labour be largest party at next UK General Election?
forecasts.key_change: Down 3pp. Electoral Calculus places Labour 5th in seats. Reform leads at 266 seats. Labour at 18% vote share, behind Reform (26%), with Greens taking 68 Labour seats.
forecasts.base_rate: Incumbent governing parties have won ~60% of UK general elections since 1945

factors_up.factor: 3+ years until election; polls can shift significantly
Reform may struggle with local organisation
Labour retains structural advantages in urban seats

factors_down.factor: Labour now 5th in MRP seat predictions
Reform sustaining 26% vote share lead
Green Party predicted to take 68 seats from Labour
CPI at 3% undermining economic credibility

key_uncertainties: Whether Reform poll lead translates to seats under FPTP

update_triggers.up: Labour recovers to >22%; Reform falls below 20%
update_triggers.down: Labour falls below 15%; major cabinet resignations

forecasts.question: Will AGI be developed before 1 January 2035?
forecasts.key_change: Stable. Longer timeline absorbs near-term uncertainty. Expert median estimates clustering around 2030-2035 range.

factors_up.factor: Capability improvement accelerating across labs
Massive investment ($100B+) in AI compute
Multiple credible experts predicting AGI before 2030

factors_down.factor: Definitional ambiguity may prevent clear resolution
Potential regulatory slowdowns
Historical AI winter cycles

key_uncertainties: Whether current paradigm achieves true AGI

update_triggers.down: Major scaling plateau or regulatory moratorium

forecasts.question: Will China initiate a military blockade or invasion of Taiwan before 1 January 2030?
forecasts.key_change: Stable. PLA conducted two-day exercises in early April with hypersonic missiles, but ASPI assesses 2026 is not the year. Gray zone preferred over kinetic action.
forecasts.base_rate: Base rate for great power military confrontation ~2-5% per year

factors_up.factor: PLA exercises intensifying with hypersonic missiles
US-China relations tense; trade war ongoing
Xi consolidating power with fewer checks

factors_down.factor: Economic cost estimated in trillions
PLA logistics and amphibious gaps persist
Gray zone coercion preferred
ASPI: 2026 is not the year

key_uncertainties: Whether Taiwan political crisis triggers opportunistic action

update_triggers.up: Major Taiwan political crisis; PLA mobilisation beyond exercises

forecasts.question: Will UK annual CPI inflation fall below 2.0% for at least one month before 1 January 2027?
forecasts.key_change: Stable. CPI stuck at 3.0% for Jan and Feb 2026. Only ~8 monthly readings remain. Would need 1pp+ drop — extremely unlikely without major deflationary shock.
forecasts.base_rate: UK CPI below 2% in ~30% of months since 2000, but current trend firmly above

factors_up.factor: Energy price falls could mechanically reduce CPI
Global recession could create deflationary pressure

factors_down.factor: CPI at 3.0% needs >1pp drop in remaining months
Services inflation remains sticky above 4%
Only 8 readings remain before deadline

key_uncertainties: Whether energy prices fall sharply enough
Impact of employer NICs on services inflation

forecasts.question: Will Spider-Man: Brand New Day gross over $1.5B worldwide by 31 December 2026?
forecasts.key_change: Up 2pp. Trailer became first ever to hit 1B views in 4 days. Industry projections at $2.5B worldwide. Opening expected to rival Endgame.
forecasts.base_rate: Spider-Man: No Way Home grossed $1.9B; 7 MCU films crossed $1.5B

factors_up.factor: First-ever 1B trailer views in 4 days
Industry projections at $2.5B worldwide
Tom Holland/Zendaya at peak star power

factors_down.factor: Summer competition with Avengers: Doomsday
Risk of audience fatigue if reviews disappoint
Economic disruption could dampen attendance

update_triggers.up: Advance ticket sales break records; positive screenings
update_triggers.down: Poor early reviews or test screenings

forecasts.question: Will OpenAI and Anthropic have a combined ARR exceeding $70B by 31 December 2026?
forecasts.key_change: Down 2pp. Combined ARR now $44B (OpenAI $25B + Anthropic $19B). Need $26B more in ~9 months. Growth rates expected to slow. Anthropic ARR includes gross cloud billings.
forecasts.base_rate: Combined $44B ARR as of March 2026; both on hypergrowth

factors_up.factor: Anthropic growing ~10x/year; could reach $30-35B by Q4
OpenAI projecting $30B+ for 2026; Q4 ARR could be $35-40B
Enterprise adoption accelerating

factors_down.factor: Growth rates expected to decelerate
Anthropic ARR inflated by gross cloud billings
Competition from Google/Meta could cap growth
$26B gap to close in 9 months

update_triggers.up: Either company reports Q2 ARR above trend
update_triggers.down: Major contract losses or growth deceleration

forecasts.question: Will UK average annual real GDP growth exceed 1.5% across the 2030s (2030-2039)?
forecasts.key_change: Stable. OBR March 2026 forecasts growth averaging 1.6% from 2027, just above threshold. Structural headwinds from ageing population and high debt persist.
forecasts.base_rate: UK average GDP growth ~1.8% in 2010s, ~2.3% in 2000s

factors_up.factor: OBR medium-term forecast averages 1.6%
AI productivity gains could boost growth 0.5-1pp
Planning reform and infrastructure investment

factors_down.factor: Ageing population reduces labour force growth
High public debt (95% GDP) constrains fiscal stimulus
Post-Brexit trade friction
Current 2026 forecasts of 1.1-1.3% show weakness

key_uncertainties: Whether AI productivity gains materialise at scale in UK
